title: SurrogateRun
description: A FAMILY-WISE surrogate run — the methodological hero, and the decisive 2026-06 audit upgrade. For a SEARCHED grid the correct null is the family-wise MAX-statistic: draw a surrogate panel, rebuild EVERY config on it, take the grid-MAXIMUM score per surrogate, and compare the REAL grid-best against that distribution of grid-maxima (surr95). A single-best-config p is wrong and overstates significance: several earlier PASS leads flipped to KILL under the right null (real-best < surr95). This schema records the surrogate method, the grid size, the real grid-best, the surrogate grid-maxima distribution, and the resulting family-wise p.

surrogateMethod.description: The null generator, matched to the claim (time-series timing -> phase/block; rotation / lead-lag -> cross-sectional shuffle; vol-clustering -> GARCH; bracket overlays -> bracket-on-surrogate).

familyWise.description: Must be true: a searched grid requires the family-wise MAX-statistic. A single-best-config surrogate is not a valid null for a searched grid.

gridSize.description: Number of distinct configs rebuilt on EACH surrogate draw (the family). The honest N for the max-statistic — not 1.

statistic.description: Statistic the surrogate null is scored on. Defaults to sharpe for structure/temporal edges.

realBest.description: The REAL grid-best score (the selected config's score on real data).

surrogateMaxima.description: The grid-MAXIMUM score from each surrogate draw (one per iteration). The family-wise null distribution.

surr95.description: 95th percentile of the surrogate grid-maxima. The bar the real grid-best must beat. If realBest < surr95 the lead fails the right null.

placeboP.description: Family-wise p = fraction of surrogate grid-maxima >= realBest (with a +1/+1 small-sample correction). Must be below the bar (default 0.05) to pass.

pass.description: True iff realBest beats the family-wise null (realBest > surr95 and placeboP < bar). A PASS proves the structure/sign is non-random; it does NOT prove tradable magnitude.

seed.description: Seed for the surrogate draws, so the run is deterministic and reproducible.
